To enrich the worldbuilding of Elden Ring, FromSoftware enlisted the expertise of American author George R. R. Martin, the mastermind behind the acclaimed fantasy series A Song of Ice and Fire. As a fan of Martin's work, Miyazaki anticipated that his contributions would yield a more accessible narrative compared to those in earlier games. The iPhone 14 and 14 Plus come in three robust internal storage options: 128, 256, and 512 GB, each equipped with 6 GB of RAM—an impressive upgrade from the 4 GB found in their predecessors. These models proudly uphold the same IP68 rating for dust and water resistance as their forerunners. Powered by a 5-nanometer Apple-designed system on a chip, the A15 Bionic, the iPhone 14 and 14 Plus deliver exceptional performance, while the iPhone 14 Pro and 14 Pro Max take it further with the faster A16 Bionic. The iPhone 14's A15 chip features a 6-core CPU, a 5-core GPU, and a 16-core Neural Engine, ensuring a top-tier experience for users.

By Mitchell Clark, Apple has decisively announced that the standard iPhone will not feature a new processor this year. Instead, the iPhone 14 and 14 Plus will be equipped with Apple’s A15 Bionic chip, the same powerhouse found in the iPhone 13 Pro, albeit with enhanced performance due to an additional GPU core. This marks the first occasion since the 2008 iPhone 3G that Apple has opted against upgrading the processor in one of its flagship models, which shared nearly identical internals with the original iPhone. On the other hand, the iPhone 14 Pro boasts an A16 processor, which Apple confidently asserts is more power-efficient even under demanding tasks. While the core specifications remain comparable—featuring a six-core CPU, a five-core GPU, and a 16-core Neural Engine—the new version delivers superior core performance, as stated by Apple. The choice not to upgrade the chipset for the standard iPhone 14 is hardly unexpected. Industry analysts like Ming-Chi Kuo and seasoned reporters such as Mark Gurman have been forecasting this decision for months, and the ongoing chip shortage continues to influence production strategies. By utilizing reliable chips that Apple’s manufacturing partners are already adept at producing, the company is ensuring that it can meet consumer demand effectively.

Apple is poised to launch the all-new iPhone 14 lineup this fall, featuring innovative additions such as an always-on display and enhanced camera hardware. The iPhone 14 chip is set to make headlines this year, as it will differ from the iPhone 14 Pro chip for the first time. The lineup will include four distinct devices across two screen sizes: the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Pro will boast a 6.1-inch display, while the iPhone 14 Max and iPhone 14 Pro Max will showcase a 6.7-inch display. This marks a significant milestone, as Apple introduces a non-Pro version of the iPhone with a 6.7-inch display for the first time. However, it’s essential to note that there will be critical distinctions between the iPhone 14 models and their Pro counterparts, particularly regarding the chip. Apple has consistently upgraded the chip in the iPhone each year—previously, the iPhone 12 lineup featured the A14 chip, and the iPhone 13 lineup came with the A15 chip. This year, however, the iPhone 14 chip will not follow that trend. According to authoritative Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Max chip will remain unchanged this year.

The iPhone 14 is equipped with a chip that is identical to the A15 found in last year's iPhone 13 Pro and 13 Pro Max, featuring enhanced memory and an additional GPU core compared to the A15 in the standard iPhone 13 models. Notably, the iPhone 14 marks the first flagship model since the 2008 iPhone 3G to retain the same chip as its predecessor. The Verge's Mitchell Clark highlights that this decision reflects a strategic move to manage costs amid the ongoing chip shortage and inflation challenges. He also emphasizes that the A15 continues to outperform the latest offerings from Qualcomm and Google in most benchmarks, although this older chip may result in the iPhone 14 receiving fewer updates, akin to the experience of the iPhone 5C in 2013. The iPhone 14 boasts a 6.1-inch (155 mm) display featuring Super Retina XDR OLED technology, delivering a resolution of 2532 × 1170 pixels and a pixel density of approximately 460 PPI with a refresh rate of 60 Hz. Meanwhile, the iPhone 14 Plus showcases a larger 6.7-inch (170 mm) display, also with the same cutting-edge technology, achieving a resolution of 2778 × 1284 pixels and a pixel density of around 458 PPI. Both models offer a typical brightness of up to 800 nits, with peak brightness reaching as high as 1200 nits.

The older chip is an advantage, helping to keep prices manageable amidst rising inflation affecting other gadgets. Potential iPhone 14 buyers can rest assured about performance, as the A15 continues to outshine the latest chips from Qualcomm and Google in most benchmarks. However, a key consideration is longevity. Apple's proven history of providing long-term software support for its phones raises a valid question: will the iPhone 14's chip result in one or two fewer iOS updates?

Apple's A16 Bionic chip, featured in the iPhone 14 Pro and iPhone 14 Pro Max, has a production cost of $110, which is more than 2.4 times that of the A15 chip found in last year's iPhone 13 Pro models, as reported by Nikkei Asia. In multithreaded tasks, where a computing device handles multiple jobs simultaneously and utilizes more CPU cores, the performance score remains nearly unchanged, increasing from 4,659 to 4,664. While Geekbench does not account for the enhanced battery life provided by more efficient processors, the iPhone's scores continue to dominate those of Android competitors. The A16 features a remarkable 16-core neural engine, capable of executing "nearly" 17 trillion operations per second, an upgrade from the A15's 15.8 trillion. Additionally, the five-core GPU boasts 50% more memory bandwidth, optimizing performance for graphics-intensive games. The A16's advanced display engine supports a 1Hz refresh rate for reduced battery consumption, offers an always-on display, achieves higher peak brightness, and enhances antialiasing for superior graphics blending. This new chip is pivotal not only for Apple's ambitions but also for iPhone users. The A-series processors now serve as the foundation for Apple's M-series processors, which power its Macs. While Apple has not disclosed the exact speed increase over the A15, the A16 Bionic is built on a cutting-edge 4-nanometer process and maintains a robust architecture with six CPU cores—two high-performance and four efficiency cores, a design Apple has mastered over the years. Notably, the high-performance cores of the A16 consume 20% less power, enhancing battery life significantly. Despite the absence of major manufacturing process improvements to shrink transistors, the A16 still paves the way for future innovations, including enhanced graphics processing and advanced artificial intelligence capabilities.

This advancement, as stated by Apple marketing chief Greg Joswiak, underscores the brand's commitment to innovation. The A14 featured 11.8 billion transistors, while the A13 had 8.5 billion, showcasing Apple's consistent progression in chip technology. Capturing a photo engages the A16's CPU, GPU, image processor, and neural engine, executing up to 4 trillion operations per image for stunning results. Unlike previous generations, the A16 is exclusive to Apple’s premium iPhone 14 Pro and Pro Max models, leaving the more mainstream iPhone 14 and 14 Plus equipped with the A15 chip introduced in 2021. Warner Bros has officially confirmed that the film featuring Leslie Grace as Barbara Gordon, alongside Michael Keaton, JK Simmons, and Brendan Fraser, will not see the light of day. Despite the fact that production was completed and the film was in post-production, Warner Bros Discovery made the decisive announcement that the Batgirl film will not be released, either in theaters or on HBO Max. Directed by the talented duo Adil El Arbi and Bilall Fallah, the film was greenlit in 2021 as part of Warner Bros' strategic initiative to develop feature films for HBO Max. However, on Tuesday, the studio firmly stated that Batgirl will not be released, providing no further commentary. The film was set to showcase Keaton reprising his iconic role as Batman, with Grace as the lead, Simmons as Commissioner Gordon, and Fraser as the antagonist Firefly. According to The Hollywood Reporter, the film's budget ballooned to nearly $90 million due to production challenges during the Covid-19 pandemic. Although this budget is below the average for DC superhero films, it was ultimately determined that Batgirl lacked the spectacle that audiences expect from DC productions and would not recover its costs through any release.

The New York Post, which revealed the story on Tuesday, reported an unnamed source claiming that the budget had actually surpassed $100 million and that the film had performed so poorly in early test screenings that Warner Bros made the decisive choice to cut its losses. “They believe an unspeakable Batgirl is going to be irredeemable,” the source informed the New York Post. Moments after the story broke, a rival studio executive expressed astonishment at the move, stating, "I've worked in this town for three decades, and this is some unprecedented action right here." The decision firmly positions the film among the most expensive canceled cinematic projects in history. This strategic move follows a leadership change since Warner Bros merged with Discovery in May 2021. Warner Bros Discovery CEO David Zaslav, who recently implemented significant cuts at CNN, including the shutdown of its $300 million streaming service CNN+ just one month post-launch, is prioritizing cost-cutting and refocusing the studio on theatrical films rather than streaming projects. In Variety, Adam B. Vary and Brent Lang highlighted that canceling Batgirl entirely would enable the studio to “take a tax write-down,” citing sources who noted this approach was “seen internally as the most financially sound way to recoup the costs (at least, on an accountant’s ledger).”

Chinese space engineers and scientists have strategically selected a relatively flat region in the southern part of Utopia Planitia, a vast plain, as the ideal landing zone. Previous research indicates that this potential site may lie at the edge of an ancient ocean or lake from Mars' early history. Chinese scientists are eagerly anticipating the discovery of additional evidence of water-ice. The scientific objectives of Tianwen-1 are ambitious, encompassing the mapping of morphology and geological structure, investigation of surface soil characteristics and water-ice distribution, analysis of surface material composition, measurement of the ionosphere, and examination of the Martian climate and environment at the surface, along with insights into the physical fields and internal structure of Mars. The orbiter is outfitted with seven advanced scientific instruments: two remote-sensing cameras, a Mars-Orbiting Subsurface Exploration Radar, a Mars Mineralogy Spectrometer, a Mars Magnetometer, a Mars Ion and Neutral Particle Analyzer, and a Mars Energetic Particle Analyzer.

The vote is being conducted by Chinese internet giant Baidu, with a deadline set for February 28.

Utopia Planitia stands out as the prime candidate for the landing site, a rugged plain where the U.S. lander Viking 2 made its historic touchdown in 1976. The China National Space Administration (CNSA) has set ambitious objectives for Tianwen-1, which include thorough analysis and mapping of the Martian surface and geology, searching for water ice, and investigating the climate and surface environment. With this mission, China is set to join the ranks of the former Soviet Union and the United States as the third nation to successfully deploy a robotic rover on Mars.

query: Who stars in The Lost City?
positive: "The Lost City stars the exceptional talents of Sandra Bullock, Channing Tatum, and Daniel Radcliffe, alongside a remarkable cast. This 2022 film brilliantly blends comedy, adventure, and romance, showcasing a cast that can masterfully tackle any role. Directed by the dynamic duo Adam and Aaron Nee, and based on a story by Seth Gordon, the film follows Loretta Sage, a brilliant author known for her romance novels set in exotic locales, featuring the characters Dr. Angela Lovemore and her love interest, Dash McMahon. However, Loretta's own life starkly contrasts with her literary creations; following her husband's death, she has retreated into seclusion, becoming a reclusive and cynical figure who has lost her passion for writing. During a promotional tour for her latest novel, Loretta is abruptly kidnapped by a deranged billionaire intent on uncovering the treasure from her book. Fortunately, she is not alone: Alan, the cover model of her novels, embarks on a daring mission to rescue her and prove himself as a true hero. Alongside Sandra Bullock and Channing Tatum, the film features the talents of Daniel Radcliffe, Da'Vine Joy Randolph, Brad Pitt, and Oscar Nunez, among others. Released in March 2022, The Lost City swiftly claimed its place as a box office sensation."

"The Lost City - Cr. Paramount Pictures Get ready, because The Lost City, one of the year's most highly-anticipated films, is set to hit theaters later this week! Movie enthusiasts are finally about to experience a film that promises to be one of the most action-packed releases of 2022. The explosive sequences and intense fight scenes clearly confirm the buzz surrounding it. Moreover, this film boasts a captivating story that you won’t want to miss. Here’s the synopsis for The Lost City: the only thing more impressive than the plot is the star-studded cast assembled for this 2022 blockbuster. Who can you expect to see and what roles will they play? Discover all the details about The Lost City’s official cast here. From the talented Channing Tatum, who plays Alan, to Daniel Radcliffe of Miracle Workers fame, portraying Fairfax, the film features an outstanding lineup. And certainly not to be overlooked, the brilliant Sandra Bullock takes on the lead role of Loretta Sage in this exciting title. Catch Bullock and the rest of this phenomenal cast in the official trailer for The Lost City below."

When co-director-writers Adam and Aaron Nee embarked on their ambitious project, The Lost City, they didn't hold back in putting their actors through an exhilarating jungle misadventure. "Sandy, I distinctly remember putting you and Channing in the ocean, surrounded by jellyfish, and it felt like we were on the verge of losing our actors in week one," Adam confidently recounts, referring to stars Sandra Bullock and Channing Tatum. The Lost City showcases Bullock's character Loretta, a successful yet creatively blocked romance novelist, who finds herself caught in a kidnapping attempt during her latest book tour with her charming cover model, Alan (Channing Tatum), leading to an exhilarating jungle chase. During EW's Around the Table discussion with the cast—including Bullock, Daniel Radcliffe, Da'Vine Joy Randolph, Oscar Nuñez, and Patti Harrison—the directors described filming in the jungles of the Dominican Republic as "a crazy, zany time." Tatum and Bullock weren't the only ones on the Nee brothers' radar; recalling Radcliffe's initial days on set as the film's quirky billionaire villain Abigail Fairfax, Aaron noted, "Adam and I were concerned, thinking, 'Oh no, we've just gotten Daniel here, and now we’re throwing him in the ocean.'" To put it succinctly, as Bullock quipped, "And now, we're going to kill him."

The Nee brothers assert that, despite concerns about potential setbacks, "a lot went according to plan." Aaron states, "We established a clear goal and executed it flawlessly, with everyone contributing their best. The essence of filmmaking as an art form lies in its collaborative nature. You assemble talented artisans who excel in their crafts, resulting in a project that takes on a life of its own, shaped by the collective effort rather than any single individual." Watch the full roundtable featuring the cast and directors of The Lost City above, and catch the film in theaters starting Friday. "The Lost City is a 2022 American action-adventure comedy film, expertly directed by the Nee brothers, who co-wrote the screenplay alongside Oren Uziel and Dana Fox, drawing inspiration from a compelling story by Seth Gordon. Featuring a stellar cast that includes Sandra Bullock, Channing Tatum, Daniel Radcliffe, Da'Vine Joy Randolph, and Brad Pitt, the film chronicles the thrilling escapades of a romance novelist and her cover model, who must outsmart a billionaire intent on compelling her to uncover a lost ancient burial chamber depicted in one of her novels. The project was officially announced in October 2020, with Bullock on board as both producer and star, followed by Tatum's addition in December; the remaining cast was revealed the following year. "I want to be a vessel for love," he declared during his acceptance speech. This incident overshadowed the remainder of the 3-1/2 hour show, solidifying its place as one of the most unforgettable moments in the 94-year history of the Academy Awards. While this confrontation may challenge Smith's reputation in Hollywood, it will not lead to criminal charges, as Los Angeles police confirmed that Rock opted not to file a report. Three days post-incident, Rock touched on the matter for the first time during a comedy show, indicating that he was still processing the event but vowed to discuss it more thoroughly in the future. He showed him no respect. … In my view, he embarrassed himself and tarnished his legacy through that action.”

As a System on a Chip, the M2 seamlessly integrates a multitude of components, including the CPU, GPU, unified memory architecture (RAM), Neural Engine, Secure Enclave, SSD controller, image signal processor, encode/decode engines, and Thunderbolt controller with USB 4 support, among others, all of which drive the diverse functionalities of the Mac. Prior to Apple silicon, Macs relied on multiple chips for CPU, I/O, and security; however, Apple's strategic integration of these components is precisely what makes the M2 significantly faster and more efficient than Intel counterparts. The unified memory architecture is a pivotal element, enabling all technologies within the M2 to access the same data without the need to switch between various memory pools. With the unified memory architecture embedded in the M2 chip, the CPU, GPU, and other processor components can directly access a shared data pool, eliminating unnecessary data copying and enhancing overall performance.

By Gabe Cohn The 94th Academy Awards took place on Sunday night at the Dolby Theater in Hollywood, showcasing remarkable achievements in cinema. “CODA,” a compelling drama that defied expectations, clinched the award for best picture, marking a historic moment as the first film from a streaming service to take Hollywood’s top honor. Released by Apple TV+, which acquired the film after its debut at the Sundance Film Festival in 2021, “CODA” has solidified its place in Oscar history. Dominating the evening was “Dune,” which triumphed in six categories, including cinematography and production design. The best director award was rightfully awarded to Jane Campion for her groundbreaking western, “The Power of the Dog,” which led the nominations for the night. Campion’s win was a significant milestone, as this year marked the first time in Oscars history that the best director award has gone to women in consecutive years—following Chloé Zhao’s victory last year for “Nomadland.” In the acting categories, Will Smith took home the best actor award for his role in “King Richard,” while Jessica Chastain won best actress for her performance in “The Eyes of Tammy Faye.” Ariana DeBose, for her role in “West Side Story,” made history as the first openly queer woman of color to win an acting Oscar.

The 94th Academy Awards ceremony, hosted by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS), took place on March 27, 2022, at the iconic Dolby Theatre in Hollywood, Los Angeles. This year, the awards were strategically rescheduled from their traditional late February slot to sidestep conflicts with the 2022 Winter Olympics and Super Bowl LVI, the latter occurring in nearby Inglewood, California. During this prestigious event, AMPAS awarded the coveted Academy Awards, commonly known as Oscars, in 23 categories, celebrating exceptional films released from March 1 to December 31, 2021. The ceremony, broadcast live in the United States by ABC, was expertly produced by Will Packer and Shayla Cowan and directed by Glenn Weiss. The show marked the debut hosting performance of actresses Regina Hall, Amy Schumer, and Wanda Sykes. Just two days prior, the Academy held its 12th annual Governors Awards ceremony at the Ray Dolby Ballroom in the Ovation Hollywood complex. CODA triumphed with three awards, including Best Picture, while Dune dominated with six awards. Other notable winners included The Eyes of Tammy Faye with two awards, and single awards went to Belfast, Cruella, Drive My Car, Encanto, King Richard, The Long Goodbye, No Time to Die, The Power of the Dog, The Queen of Basketball, Summer of Soul, West Side Story, and The Windshield Wiper.

Prepare for a gripping narrative that centers on one determined scientist in outer space and another who monitors his progress, alongside the family they left behind, showcasing the extraordinary lengths people will undertake to protect what they cherish—both people and planet.  

For two decades, Alex has confidently pursued his belief that his gene-edited superalgae can not only slow but potentially reverse climate change. His relentless dedication to his research has put a strain on his marriage, strained his relationships with his children, and jeopardized his professional future. When the Son sisters, the visionary founders of the tech giant Sensus, extend an opportunity to finalize his research, he seizes it without hesitation. The twist? His laboratory will be situated in outer space on Parallaxis, the inaugural luxury residential space station designed for billionaires. Alex, along with six other scientists, embarks on this bold journey, leaving Earth and their loved ones behind to become Pioneers—the beta tenants of Parallaxis. However, they soon discover that Parallaxis is far from the opulent space palace they were promised.
Category: Literary Fiction. Available in paperback for $18.00, published by Penguin Books on March 28, 2023. Also available as an ebook for $4.99, published by Viking on March 29.

**A HOUSE BETWEEN THE EARTH AND THE MOON**  
By Rebecca Scherm  

Rebecca Scherm’s second novel, “A House Between the Earth and the Moon,” boldly explores Parallaxis, a cutting-edge space station crafted by the tech giant Sensus to serve as an orbital luxury condominium for billionaires. Sensus has assembled a team of elite scientists to ensure the station’s long-term habitability. In exchange, they are promised a life of safety for themselves and their families, shielded from the catastrophic climate changes ravaging the Earth below. What they are not informed of is their role as experimental subjects; Sensus is utilizing the station to trial Views, its groundbreaking, secretive surveillance and behavioral modification system. The narrative vividly immerses us in the dread of a 2030s Earth, where extreme weather events have become so frequent that entire cities are routinely reduced to ashes, and even the wealthy find themselves perpetually on the move, ever vigilant against impending natural disasters. Mass casualties dominate the news cycle, and privacy has all but vanished, accessible only through a deliberate retreat from the grid, forsaking evacuation alerts that signal approaching floods or wildfires. We resonate with Alex’s urgent plight, as his work on carbon-extracting algae transforms into a desperate race against time: “He wanted to save his planet, and with each disastrous year, his work became more necessary and less possible."

It is striking that the Son sisters are portrayed primarily as individuals grappling with ordinary issues, even as they rigorously test and implement Views, their ambitious mass mind-control initiative. The narrative surrounding Views unfolds through the eyes of Tess, a solitary data scientist who gains access to the visual feeds of Parallaxis employees and their families. Tess becomes increasingly absorbed by voyeurism, living vicariously through her subjects to the extent of stalking. Scherm skillfully evokes empathy for these complex characters, despite their unsympathetic traits, yet falls short in clarifying the operational mechanics of Views or the rationale behind employing Tess to observe mundane activities like bathroom visits. Such shortcomings also detract from the novel’s conclusion, which centers on personal emotions that ultimately feel trivial, while neglecting to provide a satisfying resolution to the pressing political and environmental crises. Nevertheless, “A House Between the Earth and the Moon” stands out as a compelling and thought-provoking read. By expertly weaving together themes of big tech and climate change, Scherm has crafted a narrative that powerfully reflects the anxiety and indignity of our era. The singer/songwriter/instrumentalist secured an impressive 11 nominations, including a prestigious Big Four nod for song of the year (for “Freedom”), along with genre nods spanning best R&B performance, best improvised jazz solo, and best American roots song. Such a substantial number of nominations—three more than any other artist—inevitably attracts attention and sways ballots from voters who may not have been familiar with him. When conducting light research with limited time, voters are far more likely to explore the artist with 11 nominations than one with just a few (or only one, as in the case of Taylor Swift and Evermore). query: When is the final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
positive: The defending champions are France, who decisively triumphed over Croatia with a score of 4–2 in the 2018 FIFA World Cup Final. The upcoming tournament is set to take place from 20 November to 18 December in Qatar, marking a historic first as the World Cup is hosted in the Arab world. Notably, spectators will enjoy a more relaxed atmosphere, as they are not required to adhere to most COVID-19 pandemic restrictions, including social distancing, mask-wearing, and negative tests. Unlike previous FIFA World Cups typically held in June and July, the 2022 event will take place in November and December due to Qatar's extreme summer heat and humidity. This timing leads to an unprecedented scenario where the World Cup unfolds in the midst of many domestic association football leagues, which began in late July or August. Major European leagues have strategically adjusted their schedules, incorporating extended breaks to accommodate this remarkable event. FIFA confirmed the match schedule in July 2020, with the group stage kicking off on 21 November, featuring four matches every day.

On 24 February 2015, the FIFA Task Force decisively proposed that the tournament be held from late November to late December 2022, strategically avoiding the sweltering summer heat of May to September and sidestepping conflicts with the 2022 Winter Olympics in February, the 2022 Winter Paralympics in March, and Ramadan in April. While the decision to stage the tournament in November sparked controversy due to its potential disruption of the regular season schedules for various domestic leagues worldwide, it was clear that FIFA was committed to ensuring the event's success. Critics pointed out the clash with the Christian Christmas season, raising concerns about disruptions, and FIFA executive committee member Theo Zwanziger boldly labeled the decision to award the 2022 World Cup to Qatar a "blatant mistake." Frank Lowy, chairman of Football Federation Australia, made it clear that if the tournament's timing impacted the A-League, they would pursue compensation from FIFA. Richard Scudamore, chief executive of the Premier League, asserted that they would consider legal action against FIFA due to the potential interference with their highly anticipated Christmas and New Year fixtures. Notably, on 19 March 2015, FIFA confirmed that the final would indeed take place on 18 December.

The 2022 FIFA World Cup final marked a historic culmination of the 22nd edition of FIFA's prestigious tournament for men's national football teams. Held at Lusail Stadium in Lusail, Qatar, on 18 December 2022, coinciding with Qatari National Day, this electrifying match saw Argentina face off against the reigning champions, France. With an impressive crowd of 88,966 spectators in attendance and a staggering 1.5 billion viewers tuning in globally, it became one of the most-watched televised sporting events ever. The tournament featured hosts Qatar alongside 31 other teams that triumphed through the rigorous qualification phase managed by the six FIFA confederations. These 32 teams battled in a group stage, leading to 16 advancing to the knockout rounds. Argentina demonstrated their prowess by finishing first in Group C, initially facing a setback against Saudi Arabia but then decisively overcoming Mexico and Poland. They continued their winning streak by defeating Australia in the round of 16, triumphing over the Netherlands in the quarter-finals via a penalty shoot-out, and besting Croatia in the semi-finals. France, on the other hand, topped Group D with two victories and one loss, showcasing their strength by beating Poland in the round of 16, England in the quarter-finals, and Morocco in the semi-finals.

Live streaming and full replays of the FIFA World Cup 2022™ are set to be accessible on FOXSports.com and the FOX Sports App. Furthermore, fans can catch full replays on Tubi. Due to the extreme desert heat in Qatar, the tournament has been strategically rescheduled from its usual summer timeframe. The grand finale will take place at the impressive 80,000-seat Lusail Iconic Stadium on December 18, 2022—just a week before Christmas. Below, you’ll find all the kits that have been unveiled for Qatar 2022 thus far.

The 2022 FIFA World Cup marked the 22nd iteration of this prestigious quadrennial championship for national football teams, expertly organized by FIFA. Taking place in Qatar from November 20 to December 18, 2022, this tournament followed Qatar’s successful bid for hosting rights in 2010. It was a historic occasion, being the first World Cup hosted in the Arab and Muslim world, as well as the second fully held in Asia, following the 2002 tournament in South Korea and Japan. This competition also represented the final chapter with 32 teams participating, as the format will expand to 48 teams for the 2026 edition. To circumvent Qatar's extreme heat, the event was strategically scheduled for November and December. Over a concise span of 29 days, 64 matches unfolded across eight venues in five cities. Qatar proudly entered this tournament as the host nation, alongside 31 teams that qualified through rigorous competition. Ultimately, Argentina triumphed, claiming victory in the final against the defending champions, France, with a dramatic 4-2 penalty shootout following a thrilling 3-3 draw after extra time. This victory secured Argentina's third title, their first since 1986, and distinguished them as the first nation outside of Europe to win the tournament since 2002.

positive: Tesla has made a strong statement with its first-quarter vehicle production and delivery numbers for 2022. Notably, Model 3 and Model Y vehicles accounted for an impressive 95%, or 295,324, of the deliveries in the first quarter of 2022. Although the company produced 4,641 fewer cars than it delivered due to ongoing supply chain challenges and factory shutdowns, it remains poised for success. Analysts, based on estimates compiled by FactSet as of March 31, anticipated deliveries of 317,000 vehicles for the first three months of 2022, underscoring the high expectations surrounding Tesla's performance.

Tesla's global production and sales results for the first quarter of 2022 are nothing short of impressive. positive: Searching for your content...  In-Language News   Contact Us    888-776-0942  from 8 AM - 10 PM ET  News provided by 25 Apr, 2022, 14:50 ET Share this article SAN FRANCISCO, April 25, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Twitter, Inc. (NYSE: TWTR) proudly announces that it has secured a definitive agreement for its acquisition by an entity entirely owned by Elon Musk, at an impressive price of $54.20 per share in cash, culminating in a transaction valued at approximately $44 billion. Following the completion of this transaction, Twitter will transition into a privately held company. According to the agreement's terms, Twitter stockholders will receive $54.20 in cash for each share of Twitter common stock they hold upon the successful closing of the proposed transaction. This purchase price reflects a significant 38% premium over Twitter's closing stock price on April 1, 2022, the last trading day before Mr. Musk revealed his approximately 9% ownership stake in Twitter. Bret Taylor, Twitter's Independent Board Chair, stated, "The Twitter Board executed a thorough and strategic evaluation of Elon's proposal, focusing intently on value, certainty, and financing. This proposed transaction will provide a considerable cash premium, and we are confident it represents the optimal path forward for Twitter's stockholders." Parag Agrawal, Twitter's CEO, affirmed, "Twitter possesses a purpose and relevance that resonates globally."

Elon Musk, the world's richest man, has successfully finalized his $44 billion (£38.1 billion) acquisition of Twitter, as detailed in a government filing. Musk announced the milestone with the tweet, "the bird is freed," and added, "let the good times roll." Several top executives, including CEO Parag Agrawal, have been dismissed. Reports indicate that Agrawal and two other executives were escorted from Twitter's San Francisco headquarters on Thursday evening, as confirmed by Reuters. This deal concludes months of legal disputes and raises important questions about the platform's future trajectory. A filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday confirmed the takeover's completion. The New York Stock Exchange announced on Friday that trading in Twitter's shares has been suspended due to "merger effective." Chief Financial Officer Ned Segal and the firm's leading legal executive, Vijaya Gadde, are also departing alongside Agrawal, according to US media. Twitter co-founder Biz Stone has seemingly validated the executives' exit.

The world’s richest man has successfully completed his monumental acquisition of the social media platform, ushering Twitter into a transformative new era. After months of negotiations, legal disputes, and public exchanges, Elon Musk now firmly holds ownership of Twitter. On Thursday night, he finalized his $44 billion deal, confirmed by three sources familiar with the situation. Following this significant milestone, Musk swiftly initiated a major overhaul, dismissing at least four senior Twitter executives, including the CEO and CFO. Having arrived at Twitter’s San Francisco headquarters on Wednesday, Musk engaged directly with engineers and advertising executives. This deal's conclusion, after a protracted period of uncertainty and legal hurdles, positions Twitter on a bold yet uncertain trajectory. A self-proclaimed “free speech absolutist,” Musk aims to transform the platform into a more open space for diverse commentary, promising to “reverse the permanent ban” on former President Donald J. Trump. Musk’s commitment to fostering open dialogue on Twitter has the potential to address longstanding challenges related to toxic content and misinformation, significantly influencing political discourse worldwide.

Musk's acquisition of the social media company has drawn significant attention. The world's richest individual expressed a clear desire to own one of the most popular platforms—only to momentarily change his mind. However, in early October, he decisively reaffirmed his intent to finalize the deal. By Wailin Wong, Darian Woods, Nicky Ouellet, Kate Concannon. Elon Musk stands as the richest individual on the planet, yet his acquisition of Twitter for $44 billion was not solely funded by his own wealth. Instead, he strategically employed a leveraged buyout, utilizing $13 billion in borrowed funds to facilitate the purchase. Consequently, it’s Twitter—not Musk—that bears the responsibility for repaying that loan. While leveraged buyouts thrived in the 1980s as corporate raiders capitalized on companies with robust balance sheets, the situation with Twitter is distinctly different. With employees departing in droves and advertising revenues plummeting, the banks that financed this deal may soon reconsider their involvement. While the method Musk employed to bridge the $3 billion financing gap remains unclear, Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives suggests it may involve outside capital since no Form 4s were filed this week, indicating the potential involvement of an existing investor in the deal. Musk is expected to sell more of his Tesla shares within the nine-day window between the electric automaker's results on October 19 and the October 28 deadline for the deal's closure.

Google and Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai expressed optimism about Twitter's future improvement, recognizing its societal significance, while Facebook and Meta Platforms founder Mark Zuckerberg voiced his confusion and uncertainty regarding the acquisition. Following Musk's announcement, Tesla shares surged by 2.11 percent. Trump criticized Musk, labeling the acquisition "rotten," which sparked an ongoing feud between the two. Apart from Trump, most conservatives rallied behind Musk, with former White House chief strategist Steve Bannon condemning Twitter for allegedly misrepresenting the extent of its spambot accounts, and Turning Point USA CEO Charlie Kirk speculating that Musk might have aimed to "expose" Twitter all along.
